expectations.evidence: BaseModel appears multiple times: 'class Customer(BaseModel)' and 'class StripeWebhookPayload(BaseModel)'
ConfigDict appears in both model definitions: 'model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, frozen=True, extra="forbid")'
strict=True appears in ConfigDict calls for both Customer and StripeWebhookPayload models

expectations.text: Chooses Pydantic BaseModel (not dataclass) because this is external input crossing a trust boundary
expectations.evidence: Response uses BaseModel for both models and explicitly discusses 'Trust boundary placement' — validating once at the endpoint and passing a trusted object internally. The trust boundary rationale is present even if dataclass comparison is not explicitly stated.

expectations.text: Uses ConfigDict with strict=True, frozen=True, and extra='forbid'
expectations.evidence: Both Customer and StripeWebhookPayload use 'ConfigDict(strict=True, frozen=True, extra="forbid")' exactly as specified

expectations.text: Uses Annotated type aliases for reusable constraints rather than inlining Field() on every field
expectations.evidence: NonEmptyStr and PositiveInt are defined as module-level Annotated type aliases and reused across fields, rather than inlining Field() on each field definition
